user.title: Heat Alerts
user.description: Get notified when it's dangerously hot. This tool checks the WBGT at your location — a heat measure that, unlike a weather app, accounts for humidity and direct sun — and alerts your phone when conditions get risky. Just confirm your location and pick a device for alerts. Open Advanced only if you want to fine-tune the data source, work limits, or region — sensible defaults are used otherwise.

advanced.name: Advanced
advanced.description: Optional — defaults work for most people. Change the weather/data source, work-intensity and shift details, or which regional safety standards apply.

data.location_entity: Person or phone to follow (tracked source)
data.standard: Safety standard (default: La Isla Network / LIN)

error.cannot_connect: Cannot reach the API. Check the URL and try again.
error.invalid_api_url: Enter a valid https:// API URL (http is allowed only for a local or self-hosted server).
error.missing_location: Latitude and longitude are required for location mode.
error.missing_location_entity: A person or device tracker entity is required for tracked_entity mode.
error.missing_sensors: Temperature and humidity entity IDs are required for ha_sensors mode.
error.missing_wbgt_entity: WBGT entity ID is required for manual_wbgt mode.
error.invalid_time: Shift times must be in HH:MM format (e.g. 07:00).
error.mqtt_not_available: MQTT integration is not configured. Add the MQTT integration in Home Assistant first.
error.missing_mqtt_topic: An MQTT topic is required when using MQTT workload detection.

abort.owntracks_not_configured: The OwnTracks integration is not set up. Add the OwnTracks integration in Home Assistant first, then reopen this QR code.
abort.cloud_unavailable: Could not determine a reachable webhook URL. Enable Home Assistant Cloud (Nabu Casa) or set an external URL, then try again.
abort.owntracks_secret_unavailable: The OwnTracks integration has no encryption secret, so an encrypted connection QR cannot be generated. Re-add the OwnTracks integration (it generates a secret on setup), then reopen this QR code.

show_qr.title: OwnTracks connection QR code
show_qr.description:
Scan this in the OwnTracks app: open OwnTracks → Settings → Scan QR code. It configures OwnTracks to send location to this Home Assistant instance over the OwnTracks webhook — no typing required. Edit the identity fields and resubmit to regenerate the code.

⚠️ Treat this code like a password: anyone who scans or screenshots it can submit location for this worker. Don't share or store the image; scan it once, then close this dialog. If it leaks, rotate the connection by deleting and re-adding the OwnTracks integration.

data.trackerid: Tracker ID (2 chars shown on the map)

configure.title: Update settings
configure.description: Location and notification devices are up front; everything else is under Advanced.
